name: generic

tension_rules.template: In {title}, a key tension is effectiveness versus generality: methods that achieve strong results on targeted tasks often sacrifice broad applicability across diverse settings.
In {title}, a core trade-off is efficiency versus quality: faster or cheaper methods tend to sacrifice output fidelity, while high-quality approaches incur greater computational or time costs.

In {title}, a recurring tension is coverage versus depth: broader surveys capture more of the landscape but risk shallow treatment of individual methods, while focused analyses miss the wider context.
In {title}, a persistent tension is novelty versus reproducibility: innovative methods push the frontier but are often harder to replicate and validate independently.
In {title}, a practical trade-off is scalability versus precision: approaches that scale to larger problems frequently sacrifice fine-grained accuracy or require approximations that erode guarantees.
In {title}, a recurring trade-off is automation versus interpretability: fully automated systems reduce human effort but make it harder to diagnose failures, verify outputs, and maintain trust.
In {title}, a central trade-off is simplicity versus expressiveness: simpler formulations are easier to analyze and implement but may lack the capacity to capture the phenomena under study.

tension_fallback: A central tension in {title} is the trade-off between {axes_hint} and what can be evaluated reliably under realistic constraints.
